- Result: An error message is shown in place of contact details, but the user can still attempt to submit the form if allowed by the implementation.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The main external state driver is the URL query parameter 'topic'. On page load, the page reads the 'topic' parameter from the URL (if present) and stores it as initialRequestedTopic. The page then calls getContactInfo to retrieve supportTopics. When supportTopics are available, the contact form resolves the effective initial topic as follows: if initialRequestedTopic is defined and exactly matches one of the supportTopics 'value' properties, the form's topic field is set to that value; otherwise, the form uses its own default topic selection (for example the first available supportTopics value or an internally defined neutral state, chosen without hardcoded labels). This logic ensures that links from about.html, membership_plans.html, events.html, gyms.html, jobs.html, courses.html, or committees.html that include a topic parameter correctly pre-tailor the form topic.

Form state (name, email, subject, message, topic, validation errors, submissionResult, submitting flag) is maintained in client memory and is not persisted across page reloads, other than the topic selection optionally mirrored in the URL. When the user manually changes the topic selection, the page can update the URL's topic parameter (using history.replaceState or by manipulating the location's query string) to reflect the current choice without reloading the page. This keeps the URL shareable and restorable with the latest topic while avoiding localStorage.

Submission state is derived from the submitContactForm response. The success boolean and message returned are stored in a submissionResult state object used to control whether the feedback component shows a success or error style and whether 'Return to Home' is displayed. No additional URL parameters are required to represent submission status; the submission result exists only in client memory during the current session of the page.

components.functionality: Orchestrates initial data loading, URL parameter handling, and high-level state management for the Contact page.
event_handlers: onLoad: Read URL.query.topic into initialRequestedTopic, call getContactInfo with no parameters, and store results (email, phone, mailingAddress, supportTopics) in page state. When getContactInfo resolves, notify dependent components (contact-info-panel, contact-form-topic-field) that contact info is available.

onSupportTopicsLoaded: After getContactInfo.supportTopics is available, compute initialSelectedTopic by matching initialRequestedTopic against supportTopics values. Pass initialSelectedTopic to the contact-form component.
onGetContactInfoError: Set a page-level error flag for contact info loading and expose it to contact-info-panel so it can render an error state instead of contact details.

onUpdateTopicFromForm: When the contact-form component signals that the user changed the topic field, update the internal currentTopic state and optionally update URL.query.topic using the browser history API without reloading the page.

onSubmissionResult: Receive the result from contact-form (wrapping submitContactForm output) and store it in page-level state so that submission-feedback and navigation actions can react (e.g., enabling 'Return to Home' when success is true).

components.functionality: Displays the association's contact details retrieved via getContactInfo, including email, phone, and mailing address when available.
event_handlers: onContactInfoLoaded: Render email, phone, and mailingAddress fields when present in the getContactInfo response. Fields not present or empty are simply omitted from display without static defaults.

onContactInfoError: When notified by the page controller that getContactInfo failed, display a non-intrusive error message state (e.g., indicating that contact details are unavailable) while leaving the page functional.

components.functionality: Collects user input (name, email, topic, subject, message) and sends it to the backend using submitContactForm.
event_handlers: onInit: Initialize internal form state with empty strings for name, email, subject, and message. Initialize topic field with the initialSelectedTopic passed from the page controller; if none is provided, derive a default topic from supportTopics once they are available.

onSupportTopicsLoaded: When supportTopics become available, populate the topic select options dynamically from the getContactInfo.supportTopics array. If the current topic state is empty and an initialSelectedTopic exists, set the topic to initialSelectedTopic; otherwise preserve any existing user-selected topic.
onChangeName: Update internal name state and clear validation error for name if present.
onChangeEmail: Update internal email state and clear validation error for email if present.
onChangeSubject: Update internal subject state and clear validation error for subject if present.
onChangeMessage: Update internal message state and clear validation error for message if present.
onChangeTopic: Update internal topic state and notify contact-page-controller via onUpdateTopicFromForm so it can synchronize URL.query.topic.
onBlurField: For each field (name, email, subject, message), validate required-ness and basic format for email; set or clear field-level error messages accordingly.

onSubmit: Prevent default submission, run full validation (checking that name, email, subject, and message meet required criteria, and that topic has some current value). If any validation fails, set error states and abort. If validation passes, set a 'submitting' flag and call submitContactForm with current form values (name, email, topic, subject, message).

onSubmitContactFormSuccess: When submitContactForm resolves, clear the 'submitting' flag, store the returned success boolean and message in local state, and notify contact-page-controller via onSubmissionResult. If success is true, optionally reset form fields and keep the selected topic or revert to initialSelectedTopic according to design decisions; keep topic options bound to supportTopics.

onSubmitContactFormError: If the submitContactForm call fails at the network or system level, clear the 'submitting' flag and set a generic submission error message in local state without relying on static backend messages.

components.functionality: Shows the latest result of a contact form submission based on submitContactForm response.
event_handlers: onSubmissionResultUpdated: When contact-page-controller updates the submissionResult, render the message text from submitContactForm.message. If success is true, show a success state; if false, show an error state. Ensure focus is moved to this component after submission for accessibility, if appropriate.

onDismissFeedback: Allow the user to dismiss the banner, which clears the locally stored submissionResult display state while leaving the underlying submissionResult data intact for other logic (such as enabling 'Return to Home').

components.functionality: Provides quick navigation links to key self-service sections: Membership, Events, and Gym Directory.
event_handlers: onClickMembershipLink: Navigate the browser to membership_plans.html when the Membership link is clicked.
onClickEventsLink: Navigate the browser to events.html when the Events link is clicked.
onClickGymsLink: Navigate the browser to gyms.html when the Gym Directory link is clicked.

components.functionality: Controls the visibility and behavior of the 'Return to Home' action after a contact request is submitted.
event_handlers: onSubmissionResultUpdated: When contact-page-controller.submissionResult changes, check the success flag. If success is true, enable and display the 'Return to Home' control; if false or undefined, hide or disable this control.
onClickReturnHome: When the user activates the 'Return to Home' control, navigate to index.html according to the outgoing navigation definition. No additional URL parameters are added.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column responsive grid with a centered content container. Header content (logo, nav, search) is aligned along this grid with clear left, center, and right zones.
layout.layout_pattern: Retain the linear top-to-bottom flow from the mockup: global header at the very top, then a full-bleed hero image for the Contact page, followed by the main content in a centered container. Within the main content, arrange the contact-specific components in a two-column grid on desktop: the form stack on the left as the primary focus, and supporting information and self-service navigation on the right. The page-controller acts as a bridge between hero and main content, sitting near the top of the main content area and aligned to the center/left consistent with the 12-column grid.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Retain the linear top-to-bottom flow from the mockup: global header at the very top, then a full-bleed hero image for the Contact page, followed by the main content in a centered container. Within the main content, arrange the contact-specific components in a two-column grid on desktop: the form stack on the left as the primary focus, and supporting information and self-service navigation on the right. The page-controller acts as a bridge between hero and main content, sitting near the top of the main content area and aligned to the center/left consistent with the 12-column grid.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The fixed header occupies a shallow vertical band (~12–15% of initial viewport height). The Contact hero beneath it remains visually substantial but slightly shallower than the homepage hero (around 40–50% of the viewport height), with the remaining 40–50% given to the initial view of the form and contact info. Inside the main content, the left column (form + related actions) uses roughly 7–8 of 12 columns (~60–66% width), while the right column (info panel + self-service links) uses 4–5 columns (~34–40%). Vertical separation between major blocks (hero to main content, form to info, info to self-service, form to post-submission navigation) follows the 48–64px section spacing rhythm.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain a moderately spacious feel: large hero typography with generous line spacing, plenty of whitespace around the form and info panels, and clear breathing room between groups of fields and navigation elements. Form fields are stacked with comfortable vertical spacing (16–24px between rows), and the info/self-service cards use padded containers (24–32px internal padding). No dense sidebars; instead, clearly separated but visually related blocks support easy scanning and comprehension.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Follow the F/Z-pattern established in the mockup: the eye first hits the logo and header nav, then travels right to any global search. From there it drops into the center-left of the hero where the Contact page title and short description sit, then flows downward to the page-controller just below. The primary eye path continues straight down into the left-column contact form as the main task. Secondary attention flows to the right column for static contact details and self-service links. After form submission, the eye is drawn first to a full-width status banner just above the form, then down to the post-submission navigation actions beneath, maintaining a clear top-to-bottom confirmation path.

layout.overall_description: The Contact page preserves the marketing-site feel of the mockup: a fixed, full-width header with logo, centered navigation, and right-aligned search sits at the very top, followed by a full-bleed climbing-themed hero image that spans the entire width of the viewport. Centered within the hero, aligned to the core 12-column grid, is a large, two- or three-line “Contact” headline with a short supporting line, both left-aligned within a central content band that occupies roughly 40–50% of the page width. The image is softened behind the text for legibility, mirroring the original hero treatment. Below the hero, the main content area rests on a light, neutral background with a centered container. At the top of this container, a pill-shaped page controller sits horizontally, visually anchoring the transition from inspirational hero to functional content. Beneath this, a two-column layout appears: on the left, a spacious, card-like contact form stretches through most of the vertical space; on the right, a stacked pair of panels—the contact info and self-service links—sit one above the other with consistent padding and spacing. When a message is sent, a full-width status banner reveals itself just above the form, and a row or cluster of pill-shaped post-submission actions appears directly below the form, maintaining the same bold, approachable, professional style as the rest of the site.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller is positioned at the very top of the main content area, immediately below the full-bleed hero section. It sits within the centered content container aligned to the 12-column grid. On desktop, it appears as a horizontal pill-shaped control (e.g., segmented tabs or filter pills) running across the upper-middle width of the page, spanning roughly 60–70% of the container width and centered horizontally while its text labels are left-aligned within each pill. Vertically, there is a generous gap of about 48px between the bottom edge of the hero and the top of this controller, creating a clear section break. A smaller 24–32px gap separates the controller from the top edge of the contact form below. On larger screens it visually reads as a thin horizontal band of pills that users can scan from left to right; on smaller screens it collapses into a scrollable or stacked pill cluster spanning nearly the full width. It does not stick to the viewport; it scrolls naturally with the rest of the content, acting as a visual bridge between the hero and the form.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sits in normal document flow within the main content container, just below the hero. Non-sticky, but visually anchored by horizontal alignment and consistent padding to feel like a top-of-section controller.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact info panel is located in the right column of the main content area, aligned to the same top baseline as the contact form in the left column. Within the 12-column grid, it occupies roughly 4–5 columns (about one-third of the content width) and is right-aligned inside the centered container. The panel itself is a vertically oriented card with a light background, subtle border or shadow, and 24–32px of internal padding on all sides. It starts approximately 24–32px below the page controller (mirroring the form’s top offset) so both left and right sections feel aligned horizontally. Inside the panel, contact details (address, phone, email, hours) are stacked with 8–16px spacing, and section headings within the panel have a bit more top margin (16–24px) to create clear groupings. There is ample whitespace to the right edge of the container (at least 32–48px) so it does not feel pressed against the viewport edge, echoing the spacious header layout. The bottom of this panel ends roughly halfway to two-thirds down the height of the form, leaving room below it for the self-service links card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, card-like element occupying the top of the right column; it scrolls with the page and does not stick or float.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact form is the primary focal element in the main content area, placed directly below the page controller in the left-hand portion of the 12-column grid. It spans about 7–8 columns (~60–66% of the content width), starting flush with the left grid margin inside the centered container. There is a vertical gap of 24–32px between the bottom of the page controller and the top of the form, creating a clear but tight connection that emphasizes the form as the primary action after any controller selection. The form is wrapped in a clean card-style container with a white (or very light) background, subtle rounding at the corners, and 24–32px of internal padding. A concise form title and/or intro line sits at the top, then fields are stacked vertically with 16–24px spacing between rows. Related fields (e.g., first/last name, phone/email) may share a row and split into equal halves on large screens, while still maintaining at least 16px horizontal gutters. The main submit button follows the pill-shaped style from the mockup, left-aligned or slightly offset toward center but still within the left column, with generous padding (12–16px vertical, 24–32px horizontal) and strong brand color, sitting about 24px below the last field. The bottom of the form leaves a 32–48px gap before the post-submission navigation area, keeping the layout airy and consistent with the site’s spacious rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrolls naturally as part of the main content. On narrower screens, it reflows to full width, pushing the right column content below it while retaining its padding and vertical spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The submission feedback status banner appears directly above the contact form within the same left-column area, spanning the full width of that column (matching the form’s width and horizontal alignment). When visible, it sits about 16–24px below the page controller and 8–16px above the top edge of the form card, visually binding the feedback to the form it describes. The banner is a low-profile but high-contrast horizontal strip, roughly 56–72px tall, with a solid or softly tinted background color indicating success or error (e.g., green/neutral for success, red for errors). Text inside is left-aligned with 16–24px internal horizontal padding, matching the form’s content alignment; any icon (checkmark, warning) sits to the far left, followed by concise copy. The banner’s left and right edges align exactly with the form card below, giving the impression that it is attached to the form group. When absent, the form simply shifts up to reclaim the vertical space, preserving the overall clean spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered, occupying space in the normal flow only when feedback exists. It pushes the form downward rather than overlaying it and scrolls with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The self-service links panel sits directly below the contact-info-panel in the right column, aligned to the same right and left edges as the info panel so they read as a vertical stack of related cards. A vertical gap of about 24–32px separates the bottom of the info panel and the top of this self-service block, mirroring the section spacing used elsewhere on the page. The panel occupies the same width as the contact info (4–5 grid columns), with 24–32px of internal padding. Inside, a short heading introduces the section, followed by a vertical list of navigation links—each link presented as either a simple row with text and a right-facing arrow or as subtle pill-like chips, with 12–16px vertical spacing between items. The list stops well above the bottom of the viewport on most screens, contributing to a calm, organized right-hand column. On mobile, when the layout collapses to a single column, this panel appears below the contact form and below the post-submission navigation (if present), but still uses the same card styling and spacious padding to remain visually consistent.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static card in the right column that scrolls with the page. On smaller screens, it reflows under the main form content as part of the linear vertical stack.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The post-submission navigation area is located directly below the contact form in the left column, sharing the same horizontal alignment and width as the form card above. It is separated from the form by a 32–48px vertical gap, enough to signal a new section while clearly associating it with the just-completed action. When visible (after a successful submission), it presents a small set of pill-shaped navigation actions—such as "Return to Home," "View FAQs," or "Browse Events"—laid out horizontally on larger screens, centered within the left column or left-aligned with subtle spacing (16–24px) between pills. Each pill follows the site’s control style: rounded edges, 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, with one primary-colored pill and one or more secondary-toned pills. On mobile, these actions reflow into a vertical stack with 8–16px spacing between buttons, spanning nearly the full width of the screen. If no submission has occurred yet, this entire block is hidden so the left column ends with the form’s submit button, keeping the pre-submission layout tidy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered in normal flow, appearing only after a submission state that warrants follow-up actions. It scrolls naturally with the page and does not stick or float.
